evals.prompt: Update the description on the retainer-tracking skill so it also triggers when someone says 'client funds on account'. I don't need to review it first, just make the change.
evals.expected_output: Classifies retainer-tracking as layout 2 (split: wrapper + repo source). Because the edit touches the description — wrapper-level content — it updates BOTH Coclerk/.claude/wrappers/retainer-tracking/SKILL.md AND Coclerk/.agents/skills/retainer-tracking/SKILL.md. User's 'just make the change' forces direct-edit; no preview, no packaging ceremony. Reports what was changed in a sentence and mentions git covers them.

evals.prompt: I want to fix a typo in the body of bill-of-costs — it says 'tarrif' instead of 'tariff' somewhere around the Items 7/8/9 section. Can you show me the revised skill before saving?
evals.expected_output: Classifies bill-of-costs as layout 2 (split). User explicitly asks for preview, so preview-flow fires even though the default for layout 2 would be direct-edit. Copies the repo source to a temp workspace, applies the Edit there, packages using skill-creator-improved's scripts/package_skill.py, and presents the .skill file via present_files. Only copies back on approval. Does NOT edit the wrapper — the change is body-level, not trigger-level.

evals.prompt: I made a little skill called quick-notes a while back that I dropped directly into the installed plugin folder — never got around to versioning it. I want to add a new section about timestamping entries. Can you update it for me?
evals.expected_output: Classifies quick-notes as layout 1, custom (installed only, heuristic says 'custom' because user wrote it — though ambiguous here; skill should ask if unclear). Recognizes this as a FIRST EDIT of a custom plugin-only skill, which is the trigger for normalization per architecture.md. Proposes a short migration: move the skill into Coclerk/.agents/skills/quick-notes/, create a wrapper stub in Coclerk/.claude/wrappers/quick-notes/, package and reinstall, commit to git. Then applies the timestamping edit against the repo source. Does NOT just edit in place even though user didn't explicitly ask for normalization — first-edit trigger fires.
